channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: With plans in the works and troops on the way, military assumes a mostly familiar role on the border
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Dozens of Marines unfurled coils of concertina wire — one on the ground and one slightly above — making it more difficult to climb a border wall separating Tijuana from San Diego.
item.dc:creator: ELLIOT SPAGAT, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Hershey isn't sure why Kisses are called 'Kisses'
item.description: Why isn't it called a Hershey’s Smooch or a Hershey’s Cone? Even the people at Hershey aren’t exactly sure.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump fires the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — President Donald Trump has fired the director of the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau, Rohit Chopra, in the latest purge of a Biden administration holdover.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Philadelphia mayor confirms 7 dead, including one on the ground, in medical transport jet crash
item.description: Six people on board a medical transport jet that crashed in Philadelphia were killed. That is according to Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum.
item.dc:creator: Michael R. Sisak, KREX 5

item.title: A medical plane carrying a child patient and 5 others crashes in Philadelphia, setting homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet carrying a child patient, her mother and four others has crashed into a Philadelphia neighborhood about 30 seconds after taking off, erupting in a fireball and engulfing several homes in flames.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Beneath the surface, Groundhog Day's deep roots predate Punxsutawney Phil
item.description: Groundhog Day predictions about the coming of spring put the spotlight on Punxsutawney Phil every Feb. 2, but celebrating the midpoint between the shortest and darkest day of the year and the spring equinox has ancient roots in European agricultural life.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Colleges around the US cautiously navigate Trump's DEI crackdown
item.description: Around the United States, colleges and universities are cautiously navigating the Trump administration's crackdown on diversity, equity and inclusion initiatives.
item.dc:creator: Maryclaire Dale, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Carolina puts inmate Marion Bowman Jr. to death in state’s third execution since September
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — South Carolina put a third inmate to death in four months Friday as it goes through a backlog of prisoners who exhausted their appeals while the state couldn’t find lethal injection drugs. Marion Bowman Jr. was executed at 6:27 p.m.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate Democrats call for reinstatement of IGs fired by Trump
item.description: The lawmakers said that Congress did not receive the 30-day notice or the reason for the IG’s removals which took place at over a dozen agencies.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WGN 9

item.title: Medical plane carrying pediatric patient crashes in Philadelphia, sets homes ablaze
item.description: A medical transport jet headed for Missouri and carrying two people and four crew members crashed in Northeast Philadelphia Friday night.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, KRON4

item.title: CBS agrees to hand over '60 Minutes' Harris interview transcripts to FCC
item.description: CBS says it will turn over an unedited transcript of its October interview with Kamala Harris to the Federal Communications Commission, part of President Donald Trump's ongoing fight with the network over how it handled a story about his opponent.
item.dc:creator: DAVID BAUDER, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: What is known about the fatal crash of a plane and Army chopper
item.description: American Airlines Flight 5342 hit an Army helicopter near Washington D.C.'s Reagan National Wednesday night, sending the two aircraft into the Potomac River and killing all 67 aboard in the deadliest U.S. air crash in more than two decades. The crash around 9 p.m.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS and HALLIE GOLDEN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NTSB determined to get crash probe right: Ex-FAA chief
item.description: An ex-FAA official does not expect the National Transportation Safety Board to rush to judgment in fatal midair crash.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation

item.title: Cartels authorized the use of weaponized drones against ICE agents
item.description: U.S. Customs and Border Protection is taking threats seriously enough to circulate memos among agents.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Tour guide at former Colorado gold mine fell out of elevator after apparently not latching door
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A tour guide killed at a former Colorado gold mine last year fell out of a crowded elevator taking tourists below ground after apparently not latching its door closed, documents obtained by The Associated Press show.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Atlanta rethinks clearing homeless camps after a man is crushed inside his tent
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — Atlanta leaders are reconsidering how they dismantle homeless camps after a man was crushed inside a tent as a bulldozer destroyed makeshift homes in preparation for Martin Luther King Jr. holiday events.
item.dc:creator: JEFF MARTIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Dell CEO says company is 'retiring' remote work, calling workers back 5 days a week: report
item.description: (KRON) -- Dell has reportedly joined the growing list of companies putting an end to remote work policies and demanding that workers be in the office five days a week.
item.dc:creator: Alex Baker, KRON4

item.title: 40+ bodies pulled from Potomac near Reagan National
item.description: More than 40 bodies have been recovered from the Potomac River after a deadly mid-air collision sent an Army Black Hawk helicopter and American Airlines plane diving into the icy water earlier this week, according to The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Flight recorders recovered in DCA crash
item.description: The National Transportation Safety Board (NTSB) said Thursday that they recovered flight data recorders, known as black boxes, from the aircraft involved in the deadly mid-air collision near Reagan Washington National Airport earlier this week.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, Fox 5

item.title: DC plane crash: What to know about the mistakes made leading up to the fatal collision
item.description: As the National Transportation Safety Board investigates the fatal midair collision that left 67 dead, details are emerging about mistakes and failures leading up to the crash.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump warns Canada, Mexico tariffs are coming on Saturday
item.description: President Donald Trump on Thursday said he plans to follow through on Saturday on his threat to impose 25 percent tariffs on goods from Mexico and Canada.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Congress authorized more flights at Reagan National despite warnings
item.description: Congress added more flights to Reagan National's schedule last year despite warnings from the airport.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, KRON4

item.title: DC plane crash: Recovery continues, NTSB says investigation ongoing
item.description: Air crash investigations can take months, and federal investigators told reporters they would not speculate on the cause.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Midair collision kills 67 people in the deadliest US air disaster in almost a quarter century
item.description: ARLINGTON, Va. (AP) — A midair collision between an Army helicopter and a jetliner killed all 67 people aboard the two aircraft, officials said Thursday, as they scrutinized the actions of the military pilot in the country's deadliest aviation disaster in almost a quarter century.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, TARA COPP, ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON and LEA SKENE, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Reagan National Airport one of world's most complex airspaces
item.description: Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport (DCA) services both military helicopters and passenger planes every day.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, Fox 5

item.title: Court says banning gun sales to young adults under 21 is unconstitutional
item.description: BATON ROUGE, Louisiana (AP) — A U.S. appeals court on Thursday ruled against a federal law requiring young adults to be 21 to buy handguns, finding it violated the Second Amendment. The ruling, handed down by a panel of three judges on the conservative U.S. 5th U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE and LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Midwest woman pardoned for Jan. 6 offenses sentenced in deadly drunken-driving crash
item.description: Emily Hernandez, a Missouri woman pardoned after her ties to the Jan. 6, 2021, insurrection at the U.S. Capitol, was sentenced Wednesday in connection with a drunken-driving crash.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, WGN 9

item.title: First officer on deadly DC plane crash identified by father
item.description: RICHMOND HILL, Ga. (WSAV) — The family of one of the DC plane crash victims has been identified by his family. Nexstar affiliate WSAV confirmed through family that a Richmond Hill High School graduate was one of the 64 people who perished on the fatal American Airlines D.C. flight.
item.dc:creator: Dylan James, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Things to know about the Trump administration order on miles per gallon for cars and pickups
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Hours after being sworn in as the new U.S. Secretary of Transportation, Sean Duffy took aim at the main way the federal government regulates miles per gallon for cars and pickup trucks — also a principal way that it regulates air pollution and addresses climate change.
item.dc:creator: ALEXA ST. JOHN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump signs Laken Riley Act, marking first legislative win of second term
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday signed the Laken Riley Act into law, marking his first legislative victory since he returned to the White House after he campaigned on immigration and the border during his 2024 campaign.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, Fox 5

item.title: Gymnast dies after falling from pole while celebrating Philadelphia Eagles win
item.description: Tyler Sabapathy, 18, "sustained critical injuries" in the fall and passed away Tuesday, Temple University announced.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Yakubovsky, Fox 5

item.title: White House budget office rescinds federal funding freeze memo
item.description: The Trump administration’s Office of Management and Budget released a new memo Wednesday rescinding a controversial memo issued late Monday that froze a wide swath of federal financial assistance, which had paralyzed many federal programs and caused a huge uproar on Capitol Hill.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, ABC 4

item.title: Republican congressman suggests students get jobs to pay for lunches
item.description: Republican Rep. Ritch McCormick (R-Ga.) suggested that students should get jobs to pay for their lunches as the federal funding freeze proposed by President Trump threatens nutrition support for children across the country.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WGN 9

item.title: WATCH: Miles-long cluster of dolphins captured on drone video
item.description: A miles-long cluster of dolphins has been filmed leaping and gliding across Carmel Bay off California, forming an unusual “super pod” of more than 1,500 of the marine creatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: A$AP Rocky's accuser says he was stunned and furious when his old friend pulled a gun on him
item.description: A$AP Rocky's accuser, former friend and the key witness at his trial testified Tuesday that their relationship had been fraying for years, but he was “furious” and flabbergasted when Rocky pulled a gun on him on the streets of Hollywood.
item.dc:creator: ANDREW DALTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: New Homeland Security secretary joins federal agents to tout immigrant arrests in NYC
item.description: Homeland Security Secretary Kristi Noem joined federal agents Tuesday in New York City to announce the arrests of immigrants wanted on criminal charges, including a man accused of kidnapping who was seen on a viral video entering a Colorado apartment with reputed gang members.
item.dc:creator: DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Man stole car to smuggle migrants in trunk past Border Patrol checkpoint
item.description: A 19-year-old from Houston has been sentenced to three and a half years in federal prison for stealing a car that was used for human smuggling at the South Texas border, Acting U.S. Attorney Jennifer Lowery said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, Fox 5
